title: Lesson 22: Images in HTML (Adding Visual Content)

content.explanation:
🖼️ Why Images Matter

Websites are not just text — they are visual experiences.

Images help explain ideas, improve design, and make websites more engaging.

🌐 Real Use Cases

  • Profile pictures
  • Product images
  • Blog thumbnails
  • Portfolio projects

⚡ Core Idea

Images are added using the <img> tag.

syntax_breakdown.content:
🧪 Basic Image Structure

<img src="image.jpg" alt="Description of image">

📌 Key Attributes

  • src → path to image file
  • alt → description if image fails to load

🧠 Important Rule

👉 The <img> tag is self-closing (no closing tag)

👉 Always include alt text for accessibility

editor_sandbox.starter_code:
My Profile

This is my personal website profile page.

mini_challenge.instruction: 🎯 Mission: Add an image to your page 1. Add an tag below the paragraph 2. Use any placeholder image URL like: https://via.placeholder.com/300 3. Add alt text describing the image 🔥 This makes your page visual and professional
mini_challenge.hint: 👉 Example: Remember: no closing tag needed

quiz_engine.question: Which attribute is required to specify the image file location in HTML?
quiz_engine.explanation_feedback: Correct 🎉 The src attribute defines the path or URL of the image to be displayed.
